summary: NadaRuns launch-readiness FRONTEND pass on web preview (mobile viewport 390x844). Core flows mostly PASS: driver login, KYC approved screen, wallet balance display, shipper login + home, shipper-create wizard rendering, and the state-machine UI correctly advances exactly one step per tap (accepted -> enroute_pickup -> arrived_pickup via advance-button). At arrived_pickup the UI correctly gates pickup behind a swipe-to-confirm that opens an OTP modal (demo code shown in modal) and refuses to skip states. CRITICAL bug found: JWT is NOT persisted to localStorage/AsyncStorage on web — any cold deep-link to an authenticated screen (e.g. /driver-vehicles) calls /api/driver/me with no Authorization header, gets 401, and the screen stays stuck on its loading spinner forever (driver-vehicles never renders, only ActivityIndicator). Same risk applies to /wallet, /kyc, /order on a fresh tab/reload. Secondary: WebSocket /ws/driver/{id} returns 502 (handshake fails) — real-time updates dead on web preview. shipper-create has zero testIDs which hampers QA. Two seeded shipper accounts coexist: 'Demo Logistics Co' (35 shipments) and 'Demo Business' (0) — inconsistent. Numerous deprecated RN-Web prop warnings (shadow*, pointerEvents). State-machine, payment authorize-test endpoint, KYC simulate-approval, and order seeding all worked at the API layer.

minor.issue: Returns 404 — only /api/driver/me exists. Mostly fine but easy to confuse with the older path.
WebSocket handshake fails with HTTP 502 from the ingress on the preview URL. Live driver tracking will silently degrade to polling on web.

ui_bugs.issue: Cold-load deep link to /driver-vehicles hangs forever on ActivityIndicator. Console shows: 'vehicles load failed Error: API /driver/me failed (401)'. localStorage.auth_token is null after reload. Root cause: getAuthToken() in src/api.ts returns the in-memory module variable; the bootstrap layout does not restore the JWT from AsyncStorage before useFocusEffect fires the request. Additionally, the load() catch swallows the error and never flips a 'failed' state, so the spinner never resolves. Fix: (a) persist+restore JWT via AsyncStorage on app start before route mount, (b) on getDriver() failure set an explicit error state so the UI surfaces 'session expired' instead of spinning. Reference: /app/frontend/app/driver-vehicles.tsx:60-63, /app/frontend/src/api.ts:179.

JWT is stored in module memory only — full-page reload or direct URL entry loses it. Same screens that polled OK in the in-app navigation flow (wallet, kyc) will fail on a refresh. Expectation: token should be persisted via AsyncStorage/SecureStore and rehydrated before any API call.

Zero testID attributes across the entire 1727-line wizard (Pickup→Dropoff→Package→Price→When→Review). The Continue and Confirm & Create buttons have no testID. Blocks automated QA of the shipper creation flow.
Same as above: no testID on root, header, add-button, vehicle cards, plate input, confirm button. Cannot drive vehicle management with automation.

ui_bugs.component: shipper-home Demo Business vs Demo Logistics Co
ui_bugs.issue: Two shipper accounts appear with the same demo credentials in different runs — 'Demo Logistics Co' (35 shipments, 7 active) and 'Demo Business' (0 shipments). Likely a duplicate seed or a separate registered shipper using the same email. Confusing for QA. Investigate /api/seed-demo + /api/auth/shipper-register idempotency.

integration_issues.issue: Swipe-to-confirm-pickup opens an OTP modal whose demo code changes per arrival (saw 5933 then 6712 on next swipe). The demo code is displayed in the modal so a real driver could enter it, but the changing value should be documented for QA. Stage stayed at arrived_pickup until correct code entered (good — illegal skipping prevented).
wss://.../ws/driver/{id} returns 502 → WebSocket handshake failure in console. Driver location updates and incoming-job push will not arrive in real-time on web preview.

Direct navigation to /driver-vehicles (and likely /wallet, /kyc, /order, /shipper-tracking on a refresh) sends no Authorization header → 401. Auth state must be rehydrated from storage at app startup before route components mount.

issues: Long-press to select address opens a map picker — fine — but the manual 'Or type address manually...' input duplicates the same field, which can be confusing. Consider showing one or the other.

react_native_issues.issue: Console spam: '"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow"' and 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'. RN-Web 0.19+ deprecations — migrate to boxShadow and style.pointerEvents to silence and ensure forward compatibility.
'Listening to push token changes is not yet fully supported on web' — push notifications cannot be tested on web preview; not blocking but worth gating push registration to non-web platforms.
'useNativeDriver is not supported because the native animated module is missing. Falling back to JS-based animation.' — performance impact on web, not a bug per se.

action_items: [CRITICAL] Persist JWT to AsyncStorage/SecureStore (and on web, localStorage) and rehydrate via getAuthToken before any /api call. Today getAuthToken() returns the in-memory module variable so every refresh / direct deep link gets 401. Add a bootstrap step in app/_layout.tsx that restores token before children mount.

[CRITICAL] In app/driver-vehicles.tsx, on api.getDriver() error set a hasError state so the UI shows 'Session expired — please sign in again' instead of an infinite spinner. Same defensive pattern needed in any screen that gates UI on `!data`.
[HIGH] Investigate why wss://.../ws/driver/{id} returns 502 on the preview ingress. If ingress doesn't proxy WS, document the limitation and ensure the client falls back to polling cleanly.

[MEDIUM] Add testID attributes throughout app/shipper-create.tsx (step inputs, Continue, Confirm & Create) and app/driver-vehicles.tsx (Add a vehicle, plate input, Confirm, vehicle card, delete) so automated QA can drive the create + vehicle-management flows.
[LOW] De-duplicate demo shipper accounts — pick one of 'Demo Logistics Co' / 'Demo Business' as the canonical seeded shipper to avoid confusion.
[LOW] Migrate deprecated shadow* style props to boxShadow and props.pointerEvents to style.pointerEvents across components to clean up console.
[INFO] OTP pickup code rotates per arrival — verify back-end intent; document for support team.
critical_code_review_comments: app/driver-vehicles.tsx (lines 60-63): `load = async () => { try { setDriver(await api.getDriver()); } catch (e) { console.warn(...) } }` — failure path silently leaves driver === null which renders a permanent spinner (line 114-115). Always promote API failure to an explicit error state in the UI.

src/api.ts (line 179): /driver/me uses an in-memory token. Any cold load (deep-link, hard refresh, opening a new tab) starts with no Authorization header. JWT MUST be hydrated from persistent storage before the first request.

app/shipper-create.tsx is 1727 lines — exceeds the 700-line guideline. Recommend splitting into step components (PickupStep, DropoffStep, PackageStep, PriceStep, ScheduleStep, ReviewStep) for maintainability and test coverage.

Swipe-confirm components on order.tsx (swipe-confirm-pickup, swipe-confirm-delivery) cannot be triggered by a simple click — need a real drag. Consider exposing an underlying button/testID that performs the same action for accessibility and QA.

No KeyboardAvoidingView reference seen on web — KeyboardAwareScrollView (from react-native-keyboard-controller) is used in shipper-create; verify on iOS native that the bottom action bar isn't covered by the keyboard.

success_rate.backend: skipped (per request — backend already PASS 28/28 in iteration_19)
success_rate.frontend: ~70% — login, KYC, wallet, shipper-home, shipper-create render, and partial driver lifecycle (accepted→arrived_pickup) all PASS. Driver-vehicles, post-pickup OTP-gated states, full shipper cancel/track flow could not be fully verified due to JWT-not-persisted bug and lack of testIDs.

seed_data_creation: Called POST /api/seed-demo (already idempotent — created:{driver:null,shipper:null}). Called POST /api/orders/seed-new-pending (created order LD-38667). Called POST /api/driver/kyc/simulate-approval (driver-001 set to approved). Did NOT call /api/payments/orders/{id}/authorize-test on a new order because the UI shipment-create flow could not be driven without testIDs in this session.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Mobile viewport 390x844 is the right Expo web frame. login flow uses data-testid email-input/password-input/submit-button. Driver lifecycle controls have testIDs: advance-button, swipe-confirm-pickup, swipe-confirm-delivery, stage-<status>. The pickup/dropoff OTP modal does NOT have testIDs on its individual input boxes — query 'input[maxlength="1"]' returns 4 inputs and the demo code is shown in the modal text 'Demo code: NNNN' (regex it before typing). Swipe gestures must be driven with page.mouse.down/move/up over the swipe-confirm-* element, not a click. The active demo order (already accepted by the demo driver) lives behind 'Resume' on driver-home (testID resume-order-button). To create a fresh pending order from the UI, the shipper-create.tsx wizard has no testIDs — either add them or POST /api/shipper/shipments via curl with the demo shipper token, then /api/payments/orders/{id}/authorize-test, then re-test driver acceptance.
rca_of_issue: CRITICAL: JWT-not-persisted-on-web. Reproduction: 1) Open https://nadaruns-logistics.preview.emergentagent.com/login on mobile viewport, 2) sign in as demo.driver@nadaruns.com / demo1234, 3) wait for redirect to /driver-home, 4) navigate (or hard-refresh) to /driver-vehicles. Observed: My Vehicles header shows but the body is a permanent ActivityIndicator spinner. Network tab shows GET /api/driver/me → 401 with body {"detail":"Authentication required. Please login first."}. Console shows the same. localStorage.getItem('auth_token') is null. Root cause: src/api.ts getAuthToken() reads an in-memory module-level variable that is repopulated only when the LoginScreen submits — full-page navigation (page.goto in Playwright, hard-refresh in browser, deep-link from email) wipes this memory. Mitigation: at app start (app/_layout.tsx top-level effect) read the persisted token from AsyncStorage (web: localStorage) and call setAuthToken() before children mount; secondarily, in any screen guarded on `!data` (driver-vehicles, possibly others) surface explicit error state so the user can see what went wrong instead of an infinite spinner.
